title: olearia quercifolia
intro: Olearia quercifolia, also known as the oak-leaved tree daisy, is a shrub species native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Olearia quercifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
